steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Romantic painters altered subjects 'to appear more beautiful or heroic than they actually were.' The blank needs a word meaning to make something seem greater or more impressive than it truly is.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘apprehend’: means to understand or perceive something. Has nothing to do with exaggerating or enlarging a subject's traits. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘rectify’: means to correct or fix an error. The Romantics weren't correcting subjects — they were embellishing them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘counteract’: means to work against or neutralize. The opposite direction from what the passage describes. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘magnify’: means to make something appear greater or more prominent than it truly is — exactly what the Romantic style did by making subjects appear more beautiful or heroic. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
